_doc: Phase 8 D-09 regression suite. Eight synthetic entries cover the failure-cause matrix the planner expects from the dev DB's currently-stuck-failed books: (1) diacritic-only title difference, (2) subtitle-only difference, (3) Last,First author swap, (4) Dice fuzzy match, (5) ambiguous (2+ candidates equally valid), (6) genuine no_match (no candidate has overlapping author), (7) parse_error trigger (malformed candidate doc), (8) network proxy (placeholder; actual classification is upstream of the matcher). The executor MAY refresh this from the dev DB when accessible; the synthetic shape is sufficient until then. expected_outcome is one of 'match', 'ambiguous', 'no_match'.

books.title: Resolução
books.failure_cause_observed: Strict path rejects because candidate title has no combining marks; NFKD normalization fixes this in the fuzzy path.

books.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
books.failure_cause_observed: Strict path rejects because candidate title is shorter; subtitle stripping in the fuzzy path matches on the prefix.

books.title: The Lord of the Rings
books.failure_cause_observed: Strict path rejects because primary author tokens are dropped (each segment < 3 chars); Last,First swap rebuilds an author token that overlaps.

books.title: Foundation Trilogy
books.failure_cause_observed: Strict token-overlap rejects on the typo; Dice >= 0.85 against the normalized title accepts.

books.title: Dune
books.failure_cause_observed: Two top-3 candidates pass the title+author rule equally; matcher must throw AmbiguousMatchError per D-05.

books.title: An Obscure Self-Published Title
books.failure_cause_observed: OL has no candidate with overlapping author; matcher must throw NoMatchError.

books.title: Some Book With Bad Data
books.failure_cause_observed: Candidate doc has empty author_name array (parse-error proxy at the matcher boundary); falls through to no_match.

books.title: Network Proxy Title
books.failure_cause_observed: Empty candidate list stands in for upstream network failure; matcher cannot accept anything and surfaces no_match. The actual network classification is upstream of the matcher, encoded in classify-failure tests.
